title: REST APIs
objectives: Design URLs, methods and status codes correctly
Version, paginate and secure a REST API

sections.markdown: REST (Representational State Transfer) is an architectural style: resources exposed at URLs, manipulated with HTTP verbs, returning representations (usually JSON). Its constraints are client-server, stateless, cacheable, layered, uniform interface, and (optional) code-on-demand.

sections.code:
GET    /v1/users              -> list
GET    /v1/users/42           -> read one
POST   /v1/users              -> create
PUT    /v1/users/42           -> replace
PATCH  /v1/users/42           -> partial update
DELETE /v1/users/42           -> remove
GET    /v1/users/42/orders    -> nested resource

sections.markdown: REST maturity (Richardson's model): Level 0 = one URL/one verb (SOAP-ish); Level 1 = resources; Level 2 = HTTP verbs + status codes (where most APIs live); Level 3 = HATEOAS (hypermedia). Level 2 is 'good REST' in practice.
Building 'RPC-in-URL': POST /doAction, PUT /processStuff. That's not REST — it forfeits caching, discoverability, and correct semantics. Model actions as state transitions on resources.

Pagination via cursor (opaque next-token) beats offset for large sets. Support ?fields= projection to shrink payloads. Return 429 with Retry-After when rate-limited. Always include a request ID header for debugging.

flashcards.back: Client-server, stateless, cacheable, layered, uniform interface, code-on-demand (optional).
GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS. POST and PATCH are not (generally).
Cursor uses an opaque token pointing to the next page — stable under inserts; offset shifts on writes.

quiz.q: Which is the best REST URL for updating a user?
quiz.explain: Nouns for resources; PUT for full replace on a specific resource.
POST creates a new resource each call; PUT/DELETE are idempotent.
429 Too Many Requests + Retry-After tells the client when to try again.

challenges.text: Design a REST endpoint set for a books catalog (CRUD + search).
Add cursor pagination + filtering with ?field= projection.
Add idempotency keys to POST /orders and handle retries safely.
Design v1 → v2 migration with dual routes + deprecation headers + sunset dates.

interviewQuestions: How would you version a REST API?
How do idempotency keys prevent duplicate charges?
